claim_text: SWE-Bench Pro contains 1,865 problems drawn from 41 actively maintained repositories.
caveats: No direct contradiction is visible in the provided evidence.
mechanism_or_bounds: This is a benchmark composition claim rather than a causal one; the evidence supports the reported size and repository count, but not broader claims about full software-engineering coverage.
contradiction_note: No direct contradiction is visible in the provided evidence.

claim_text: SWE-Bench Pro is partitioned into public, held-out, and proprietary commercial subsets, with only the public subset released and the private subsets retained to limit overfitting and enable external reporting on unreleased tasks.
caveats: A visible tension remains because results are reported on private subsets that outside users cannot directly inspect.
mechanism_or_bounds: The partitioning structure plausibly serves benchmark-control goals by separating released tasks from unreleased evaluation sets, especially for future overfitting checks, but the evidence does not show how fully this design prevents leakage or gaming in practice.
contradiction_note: A visible tension remains because results are reported on private subsets that outside users cannot directly inspect.

claim_text: SWE-Bench Pro is designed to test longer-horizon code-editing work than lightweight bugfix benchmarks, with tasks described as taking hours to days and requiring substantial multi-file changes on average.
caveats: No direct contradiction is visible, but the claim is bounded because task difficulty is inferred from reported task properties rather than from a head-to-head benchmark comparison.
mechanism_or_bounds: The hours-to-days task description, 107.4-line average reference solution, 4.1-file average scope, and minimum 10-line change threshold together support the claim that tasks are materially extended; however, the evidence does not compare this benchmark directly against all alternative benchmarks.
contradiction_note: No direct contradiction is visible, but the claim is bounded because task difficulty is inferred from reported task properties rather than from a head-to-head benchmark comparison.

claim_text: Reported agent performance drops from the public subset to the commercial subset in SWE-Bench Pro, indicating weaker performance on the commercial portion of this benchmark.
caveats: A visible tension remains because the benchmark reports cross-split differences while language coverage is limited and some major languages are underrepresented.
mechanism_or_bounds: The evidence supports a cross-split performance gap because the best reported public-set score is 23.3% while the best reported commercial-set score is 17.8%, but the mechanism behind that gap is uncertain and may reflect differences in task mix, repository type, or private-set difficulty.
contradiction_note: A visible tension remains because the benchmark reports cross-split differences while language coverage is limited and some major languages are underrepresented.
